---
title: "Getting Started"
sidebar: en
---

## Requirements

- An iOS or Android device. No account, no sign-in.
- Local-notifications permission (asked at first launch).

## Setup

1. Install the app from the store and open it.
2. The app starts on the **Today** view; preset categories are already in place.
3. Tap a category, press **Start** — the timer is running.

![Today view: tap a category and start the timer.](../_screens/android-today.png){width=320}

## Recording a session

1. On the **Today** tab, pick a category.
2. Optionally adjust focus and break duration (default: 25 / 5 minutes).
3. Press **Start**. The app notifies when the focus block ends, suggests a break, and returns to focus mode afterwards.
4. **Stop** ends the session early; the time already elapsed is saved.

## Your own categories

In the **Categories** tab you can edit names and colours. The colour is reused in lists and charts.

![Category management with a colour tag per entry.](../_screens/android-categories.png){width=320}

## Notes

- The app keeps things **local**: no cloud sync, no external backups. Sharing data across devices is deliberately not modelled.
- Timers keep running even if the app goes to the background or the system reboots — the app reconstructs state on relaunch.
- Notifications are local; no push token is sent to any server.
